stories.headline: Nvidia CEO directs $45B into 8 AI companies including OpenAI and others
stories.body:
Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia, is telling you where to invest in 2026.

He has personally directed Nvidia's capital into 8 specific companies for a combined total of over $45 BILLION.

This is where the most important company in the AI economy is putting its money.

Here’s the full list:

OpenAI: $30 billion

The largest commitment of the 8. Nvidia is funding the buildout of OpenAI's compute infrastructure from the inside. OpenAI is also Nvidia's single largest customer.

GLW Corning: $3.2 billion
Optical glass and fiber to physically connect AI clusters. You cannot move data between millions of GPUs without it.

IREN: $2.1 billion
AI cloud provider with one of the deepest power positions in North America.

MRVL Marvell: $2 billion
Custom networking chips that move data between GPUs at massive scale.

LITE Lumentum: $2 billion
Lasers and optical components for the fiber backbone of every AI data center.

COHR Coherent: $2 billion
Fiber optic transceivers that connect GPU clusters inside data centers.

CRWV CoreWeave: $2 billion
GPU-as-a-service provider. Nvidia's largest cloud customer outside the hyperscalers.

NBIS Nebius: $2 billion
AI cloud infrastructure company. Quietly building hyperscale GPU capacity for the AI labs.

Whatever Nvidia is buying is where the money is going next.

At The Assembly, we’re a team of 8 with one goal: help you find the right stocks early.

Turn notifications on so you don’t miss our alerts. This is VERY important.

If you’re not following us yet, you will regret it later.

stories.headline: Hiring AI-native investment team leads for credit, real estate, derivatives, equities, and quant roles.
stories.body:
“Hire me! I'm a great senior engineer (code monkey)” Hard pass.

Finance bro relying on his “quant guy”? Harder pass.

LLMs will out-do the best engineers and quants. That is the bare minimum.

I want people obsessed with the problem, not the code.

If you’re obsessed with credit, derivatives, real estate, or portfolio optimization, this tweet is for you.

We’re building the most AI-native investment team on Earth. Not a fund though.

I’m not sharing the who or what yet, but here's what I can say: you’ll be investing billions of dollars in your twenties, including all my proceeds from the Capital One acquisition.

A few warnings:
- 996 is easy. Here it’s closer to 8am-10pm-7 days a week. Don’t worry, I’ll be in the office before you arrive and after you leave.

-We pay really well, but we expect even more. You’ll feel overpaid compared to your friends and underpaid compared to how much we expect. And let’s be honest: nobody gets rich out of bonuses. Real generational wealth is built through tax-deferred equity.

- We expect first-principles thinking for everything. And I mean everything. If someone asks you why you’re doing something in this way, “because this is how we did it at my last firm” is not an acceptable answer.

- We expect you to be full-stack. From vision, to building to execution. There is no “support”. If you can't use claude code to support yourself, then wtf are you even doing?

- You’ll be expected to master complex concepts in a matter of days.

- We will pair you with industry veterans so you can leverage their experience. Don’t fuck it up.

Roles we’re hiring for:

- Credit/Fixed income lead - do you think that emerging-market banks bonds provide a great risk reward, but most American investors are scared of it? Talk to us.

- Real estate lead - are you up-to-date on all the new maps for OZs and have no patience for trophy assets that don’t have great after-tax yield? Talk to us.

- Derivatives lead - You're able to represent any view by stacking the right structured payoffs? Talk to us.

- Equities lead - You like stock picking? Save your energy. You deeply understand how risk models work from first principles, how optimizers are god’s gift to humanity and how every stock pick is never good or bad, it's  just part of a portfolio? Talk to us.

- Quant infrastructure lead - You’re obsessed with finding specific signals to generate alpha? Save your energy, Citadel is a better spot. You’re into building infrastructure to test new strategies, manage risk and optimize portfolios? Talk to us.

If you think you’re a good fit, email me at henrique@sharpe.com answering the following question: What about the investment process in the asset class you have the most experience with will most materially change with AI and why?

Happy memorial weekend :)

stories.headline: Japanese Yen now world's weakest currency in real effective terms
stories.body:
The Japanese Yen has just fallen below Turkish Lira in real effective terms and is now the world's weakest currency. If anyone out there is still peddling the myth that debt doesn't matter because you can always cap interest rates, show them this chart...
https://robinjbrooks.substack.com/p/fiscal-distress-in-japan

stories.headline: Judge spares teenage rapists jail time, sparking outrage from victim and attorney general review.
stories.body:
A girl who was raped by two teenage boys has told the BBC that a judge's decision to spare them jail sentences was like a "rock straight in my face".

The attorney general is to review the sentence given by Judge Nicholas Rowland, who had said on Thursday he wanted to avoid "criminalising" the "very young" boys.

Read more:

stories.headline: Elon Musk highlights Neuralink as major breakthrough for mind-controlled tech and medical miracles.
stories.body:
Elon Musk just said Neuralink is a much bigger breakthrough than most people realize

It’s now enabling people to control computers with their minds, helping paralysis patients regain independence, and potentially allowing blind people to see again

We’re entering an era where technology is starting to achieve things humanity once considered impossible - and what can be described as “Jesus-level miracles”

stories.headline: French doctors implant lab-grown human trachea made from patient's stem cells.
stories.body:
🚨 SCIENTISTS GREW A HUMAN AIRWAY USING STEM CELLS

A medical breakthrough in France is turning science fiction into reality.
Doctors successfully created a living human trachea using a patient’s own stem cells and implanted it into the body. The lab-grown airway was built on a donor trachea scaffold, then slowly transformed into living tissue the body could recognize as its own.

After surgery, blood vessels grew into the implant and the patient was able to breathe through the regenerated airway.
This technology could one day save people suffering from severe airway damage caused by cancer, trauma, or long-term ventilation.

Source : Institut Pasteur & Hôpital Foch. Medical research on tissue-engineered tracheal reconstruction and regenerative airway transplantation.

stories.headline: Terahertz laser reveals counter-intuitive quantum rotation in crystals conserving angular momentum.
stories.body:
🚨 PHYSICISTS JUST OBSERVED A STRANGE QUANTUM ROTATION EFFECT THAT DEFIES INTUITION.

In a crystal, atoms are spinning in a way that seems to break common sense  yet still perfectly conserves angular momentum.

The effect was spotted using terahertz lasers, revealing a completely counter-intuitive quantum rotation behavior that no one expected.

Why this matters:
• It challenges our basic understanding of how quantum systems rotate and interact
• The discovery was made in a material that could be used for future quantum technologies
• It shows that even “simple” quantum phenomena still hide surprises at the atomic scale

The deeper implication is mind-bending:
Reality at the quantum level keeps revealing behaviors that feel impossible  yet are completely consistent with the laws of physics.

Sometimes the universe rotates in ways our classical intuition simply cannot follow.

What other “impossible” quantum effects are still hiding in plain sight?

stories.headline: Rep. Tim Burchett says Pentagon UFO releases making disclosure mainstream
stories.body:
NEW EXCLUSIVE:
Is Pentagon’s release of UFO files waking Congress up? “Absolutely,” Burchett tells us. "Been great”

Ask a Pol UAP asks:
Since the Pentagon started releasing the ‘UFO files,’ have other members of Congress been talking to you more about it? Like, has it made it a little more mainstream?

Key Burchett:
“Yeah. Oh, absolutely,” Rep. Tim Burchett exclusively tells Ask a Pol UAP.
 ”Thanks to y’all, people like yourself, it’s been great.”

What’s next? Another hearing?
“We’ve had enough hearings. We just need disclosure,” Burchett says.
